title: Bill 96 Storefront Signage Checker
tagline: Photo audit tool for French predominant signage on Montreal retail storefronts.

jtbd.situation: When Bill 96 requires French markedly predominant on public signs from June 2025
jtbd.persona: Owner of bilingual boutique on Sainte-Catherine in the Village
jtbd.motivation: audit storefront signage before OQLF inspection
jtbd.statement: When Bill 96 signage rules apply, a Village boutique owner wants a photo audit so they fix signs before fines.

montrealAngle: Montreal retail corridors (Ste-Catherine, Mont-Royal) mix EN-heavy branding with Bill 96 predominant French rule; CBC documented business group panic.
whyNow: Rules effective June 1 2025; packaging/signage compliance still catching up May 2026.

persona.name: Independent retailer, 1-3 locations
persona.countSource: [hypothesis] Montreal retail SMBs with EN branding

competitors.name: Signage fabricators
competitors.notes: Sell new signs; no audit SaaS.

mvpScope.coreJob: Upload storefront photo, get checklist of Bill 96 signage fixes

g1_montrealAnchor.notes: Montreal retail EN/French signage tension acute.
